Caption: (A) Picture of ultrasound (US) exposure setup. The plastic balls at the water surface maintain the temperature and dissolved gas concentration in the water tank. Schematics of side views of (B) a single-layer acoustically-responsive scaffold (ARS) containing perfluorohexane (C6)-dextran488 emulsion with varying volumes of overlying media (i.e., 0, 0.5, and 2 mL), (C) single-layer ARS backed with pre-formed bubbles generated from perfluoropentane (C5) emulsions, and (D) a bi-layer ARS loaded with 0.5% (v/v) C6-dextran488 emulsion in the lower layer (i.e., ARSlower) and 1% (v/v) C6-dextran594 emulsion in the upper layer (i.e., ARSupper) polymerized in a BioFlex plate. The plate was positioned on the surface of a water tank and US was applied through the well bottom. The top view of an ARS, total well diameter (dt), well diameter exposable to US (dUS), and lateral raster spacing (dx) of the US focus are shown in panel E. Scale bar: 20 mm.

(A) Peak-to-peak RF voltage of the scattered response, and (B) corresponding integrated frequency power spectrum from acoustically-responsive scaffolds (ARSs) containing a single-payload (i.e., 0.5% (v/v) C6-dextran488) at 2.5 MHz using a passive detection method. Using piece-wise linear fitting, the acoustic droplet vaporization (ADV) and inertial cavitation (IC) thresholds for the ARS were 2.2 ± 0.2 MPa (n = 5) and 3.9 ± 0.2 MPa (n = 5), respectively, at 2.5 MHz. Similarly, the IC threshold for the fibrin-only gels was 7.8 ± 0.1 MPa (n = 5).
